framing: Recent statistics graduate applying to junior data scientist role at QuantumBlack (graduate-friendly JD style)

jd.title: Junior Data Scientist
jd.style_note: Graduate-friendly: sells the role, explains what you'll learn, loose requirements, 'Who You Are' framing
jd.about: This is an entry-level, full-time opportunity designed for recent graduates who want to apply data science to solve real business problems. You'll work alongside experienced analysts and consultants on engagements across industries — from healthcare to financial services to retail. You'll learn to translate messy real-world data into actionable insights that drive executive decisions.

requirements.text: Recently completed (or completing by summer 2026) a bachelor's or master's degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, or a related quantitative field
Strong programming skills in Python and SQL; familiarity with R or Scala is a plus
Genuine curiosity about how businesses work and a desire to learn consulting
Strong communication skills — you can explain a regression to a non-technical audience
Experience with data visualization tools (Tableau, Power BI, or matplotlib/seaborn)
Internship or project experience involving real datasets (academic projects count)
Familiarity with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure)

what_youll_do: Work with real datasets from client engagements — not toy problems
Build dashboards, statistical models, and data pipelines under guidance from senior analysts
Present findings to client teams and learn to tailor your message to different audiences
Participate in a structured 6-month onboarding program with mentorship and technical training
Work across different client engagements spanning multiple industries in your first year

expected_output.summary: Recent Statistics graduate from the University of Edinburgh with strong quantitative foundation and hands-on project experience in Python and R. Completed dissertation on predictive modeling for NHS patient readmission rates using real hospital data. Data visualization experience through academic projects and a 3-month summer internship at Monzo. Eager to apply analytical skills to data science consulting.

experience.title: Associate Data Scientist — Intern
bullets: Analyzed customer transaction data (2M+ rows) using Python and pandas to identify spending pattern segments
Built Tableau dashboard tracking weekly customer acquisition funnel metrics for product team
Wrote SQL queries against BigQuery data warehouse to extract cohort-level retention data
Presented findings on dormant account reactivation to 15-person product team

experience.company: University of Edinburgh — School of Mathematics
experience.title: Undergraduate Research Assistant
bullets: Built predictive model for patient readmission rates using logistic regression and random forest (R, tidyverse) as part of supervisor's NHS Lothian research programme
Cleaned and merged 3 anonymised NHS datasets (45K patient records, accessed under supervisor's existing ethics approval) handling missing values and date mismatches
Achieved AUC of 0.78 on held-out test set, presented results at university statistics symposium
Co-authored poster accepted at Royal Statistical Society conference (2025)

education: BSc (Hons) Statistics — University of Edinburgh (2022-2026, expected First Class)

jd_requirements: bachelor's master's degree Statistics Mathematics Computer Science Economics quantitative
Python SQL strong programming R Scala familiarity
curiosity business consulting desire to learn
data visualization Tableau Power BI matplotlib seaborn
deliberate_flaws: Only 3 months of work experience (single internship) — rest is academic
A/B testing is coursework only, not production
No consulting exposure — doesn't know what 'client engagement' looks like
R is stronger than Python — JD requires strong Python + SQL, candidate's Python is secondary
No Scala or PySpark experience (QuantumBlack's actual stack)
